infobox.key: National Union of Tailors and Garment Workers
infobox.value: (**NUTGW**) was a [trade union](/wiki/Trade_union "Trade union") in the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom").

(**T&GWU**) in 1920 with the merger of the [Scottish Operative Tailors and Tailoresses' Association](/wiki/Scottish_Operative_Tailors_and_Tailoresses%27_Association "Scottish Operative Tailors and Tailoresses' Association") and the [United Garment Workers' Union](/wiki/United_Garment_Workers%27_Union "United Garment Workers' Union"). In 1932, it was joined by the [Amalgamated Society of Tailors and Tailoresses](/wiki/Amalgamated_Society_of_Tailors_and_Tailoresses "Amalgamated Society of Tailors and Tailoresses") and renamed itself as the "National Union of Tailors and Garment Workers". In 1939 it absorbed the [United Ladies Tailors' Trade Union](/wiki/United_Ladies_Tailors%27_Trade_Union "United Ladies Tailors' Trade Union").[[1]](#cite_note-Archives-1) The NUTGW had 118,700 members in 1945, making it the tenth largest union in Britain.[[2]](#cite_note-Marsh-2) It absorbed the Manchester-based [Waterproof Garment Workers' Trade Union](/wiki/Waterproof_Garment_Workers%27_Trade_Union "Waterproof Garment Workers' Trade Union") in 1972.[[1]](#cite_note-Archives-1)

Marsh, Arthur (1979). [*Trade Union Handbook: A Guide and Directory to the Structure, Membership, Policy and Personnel of the British Trade Unions*](https://books.google.com/books?id=UEkOAAAAQAAJ). Westmead, Hants.: Gower Press. p. 14. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [0-566-02091-2](/wiki/Special:BookSources/0-566-02091-2 "Special:BookSources/0-566-02091-2"). Retrieved 16 April 2013.

Waddington, Jeremy; Kahmann, Marcus; Hoffmann, Jürgen (2005). [*A Comparison of the Trade Union Merger Process in Britain and Germany: Joining forces?*](https://books.google.com/books?id=Klj7lm7311oC). Routledge Research in Employment Relations. Abingdon: [Routledge](/wiki/Routledge "Routledge"). pp. 59–60. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [0-415-35378-5](/wiki/Special:BookSources/0-415-35378-5 "Special:BookSources/0-415-35378-5"). Retrieved 17 April 2013.
[Labour Party](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)"), *Report of the Fifty-Eighth Annual Conference of the Labour Party*, pp.179-201

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Former trade union of the United Kingdom

NUTGW

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| National Union of Tailors and Garment Workers | |
|  | |
| Merged into | [General, Municipal and Boilermakers and Allied Trades Union](/wiki/General,_Municipal_and_Boilermakers_and_Allied_Trades_Union "General, Municipal and Boilermakers and Allied Trades Union") |
| Founded | 1920 |
| Dissolved | 1991 |
| Headquarters | 14 [Kensington Square](/wiki/Kensington_Square "Kensington Square"), London |
| Location | * [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") |
| Members | 118,700 (1945) |
| Affiliations | [TUC](/wiki/Trade_Union_Congress "Trade Union Congress"), [Labour](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)") |

The **National Union of Tailors and Garment Workers** (**NUTGW**) was a [trade union](/wiki/Trade_union "Trade union") in the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Union_of_Tailors_and_Garment_Workers&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The union was founded as the **Tailors and Garment Workers' Union** (**T&GWU**) in 1920 with the merger of the [Scottish Operative Tailors and Tailoresses' Association](/wiki/Scottish_Operative_Tailors_and_Tailoresses%27_Association "Scottish Operative Tailors and Tailoresses' Association") and the [United Garment Workers' Union](/wiki/United_Garment_Workers%27_Union "United Garment Workers' Union"). In 1932, it was joined by the [Amalgamated Society of Tailors and Tailoresses](/wiki/Amalgamated_Society_of_Tailors_and_Tailoresses "Amalgamated Society of Tailors and Tailoresses") and renamed itself as the "National Union of Tailors and Garment Workers". In 1939 it absorbed the [United Ladies Tailors' Trade Union](/wiki/United_Ladies_Tailors%27_Trade_Union "United Ladies Tailors' Trade Union").[[1]](#cite_note-Archives-1) The NUTGW had 118,700 members in 1945, making it the tenth largest union in Britain.[[2]](#cite_note-Marsh-2) It absorbed the Manchester-based [Waterproof Garment Workers' Trade Union](/wiki/Waterproof_Garment_Workers%27_Trade_Union "Waterproof Garment Workers' Trade Union") in 1972.[[1]](#cite_note-Archives-1)

The NUTGW faced a long-term decline in membership over the second half of the 20th century as the number of workers employed in the British [clothing industry](/wiki/Clothing_industry "Clothing industry") shrunk. This was largely due to competition with foreign manufacturers. By 1990 membership had fallen to less than 70,000, from a peak of over 130,000 in 1950.[[3]](#cite_note-Waddington-3) The NUTGW merged into the [General, Municipal, Boilermakers and Allied Trades Union](/wiki/General,_Municipal,_Boilermakers_and_Allied_Trades_Union "General, Municipal, Boilermakers and Allied Trades Union") in 1991.[[1]](#cite_note-Archives-1)

## Election results

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Union_of_Tailors_and_Garment_Workers&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Election results")]

The union sponsored a [Labour Party](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)") Member of Parliament from 1958 until 1964.[[4]](#cite_note-lab59-4)

| Election | Constituency | Candidate | Votes | Percentage | Position |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [1958 by-election](/wiki/1958_Shoreditch_and_Finsbury_by-election "1958 Shoreditch and Finsbury by-election") | [Shoreditch and Finsbury](/wiki/Shoreditch_and_Finsbury_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Shoreditch and Finsbury (UK Parliament constituency)") | [Michael Cliffe](/wiki/Michael_Cliffe "Michael Cliffe") | 10,215 | 76.0 | 1 |
| [1959 general election](/wiki/1959_United_Kingdom_general_election "1959 United Kingdom general election") | [Shoreditch and Finsbury](/wiki/Shoreditch_and_Finsbury_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Shoreditch and Finsbury (UK Parliament constituency)") | [Michael Cliffe](/wiki/Michael_Cliffe "Michael Cliffe") | 22,744 | 67.0 | 1 |

## General Secretaries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Union_of_Tailors_and_Garment_Workers&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: General Secretaries")]

:   1920: [Andrew Conley](/wiki/Andrew_Conley "Andrew Conley")
:   1948: [Anne Loughlin](/wiki/Anne_Loughlin "Anne Loughlin")
:   1953: [John E. Newton](/wiki/John_E._Newton "John E. Newton")
:   1969: [Jack Macgougan](/wiki/Jack_Macgougan "Jack Macgougan")
:   1979: [Alec Smith](/wiki/Alec_Smith_(trade_unionist) "Alec Smith (trade unionist)")
